_meta.description: System topology: all repos, deployment config, data flow pipelines, strategy manifest, dependency graph, and UI/API mappings. Extracted from workspace-manifest.json and related PM manifests.

versions._note: All versions reset to 0.x.x (2026-02-28). Nothing was stable under the old CI/CD — versions >=1.0.0 were aspirational. 1.0.0 will be set by GitHub Action on first successful main merge per repo. 0.x.y preserves relative evolution (e.g. UTL 0.2.2 reflects more history than AC 0.1.0).

topological_order.description: Order for cascading quickmerge (dependencies BEFORE dependents). Drives CI/CD checkout order. L0-L1 are infrastructure deps (PM + codex) that ALL repos depend on for standards, templates, and manifest. CI clones these alongside the target repo.

levels.description: Project management — pure root (manifest, templates, scripts, cursor rules). No dependencies.
Standards & codex — coding standards, architecture docs, quality gate templates. Depends on PM.
Foundation — zero inter-library dependencies (Tier 0). Depends on PM + codex for CI standards.
Core interfaces (depends on UTEI, UTL, T0)
Foundational service (instruments only; no service-domain-data dependency)
Deployment infrastructure (deployment-api, deployment-service; deploys all services)
IaC + post-deploy validation (deploy last; references all service images)

0.description: Pure leaf — zero inter-library deps. Uses doc dependencies (PM, codex) for CI standards. Repos: unified-api-contracts, unified-internal-contracts, unified-events-interface, unified-cloud-interface, execution-algo-library, matching-engine-library

1.description: Services runtime (library) — imports Tier 0 only. Services use UTL for L2. Repos: unified-trading-library (UTL), unified-reference-data-interface, unified-config-interface (UCI)

2.description: Domain/market interfaces — imports Tier 0+1, NOT Tier 3. Repos: unified-market-interface, unified-trade-execution-interface, unified-ml-interface, unified-feature-calculator-library, unified-position-interface, unified-defi-execution-interface, unified-sports-execution-interface

3.description: Domain data client — cloud storage I/O, imports Tier 0+1. Repos: unified-domain-client (UDC)

service.description: Services — import from all library tiers. Never import from another service repo.

api.description: API gateway services (FastAPI+OAuth) — HTTP boundary between services and UIs. Deps: UTL+UCI+UEI only.

ui.description: TypeScript UIs — call API services via HTTP/SSE only. Never import Python libraries.

infrastructure.description: Infrastructure repos — codex, IaC (Terraform), scripts. No Python package deps. Repos: unified-trading-codex, ibkr-gateway-infra.

devops.description: DevOps/deployment — deployment-service, deployment-api. Imports UTL, UCI, UEI.

integration.description: Integration test harness — system-integration-tests. HTTP/GCS/PubSub only, no service imports.

publishing_order.description: Order for publishing packages to Artifact Registry

steps.reason: Tier 1 packages. UCI depends on UEI. UTL depends on T0 libs.
Can be published once all libraries are available

completion_paths._description: Which repos are required for each vertical completion milestone. Unblocked = can be done now; blocked = needs another path's completion first.

cefi.label: CeFi / TradFi Commercialisation
cefi.description: Minimum set for commercial CeFi trading: crypto spot+perp (Binance, OKX, Bybit, Hyperliquid) + traditional equities (IBKR).
required_libraries: unified-api-contracts (CeFi venues: Binance, OKX, Bybit, Hyperliquid, Coinbase, IBKR, Deribit, Kalshi)
unified-position-interface (v1.0.0 needed before exec-services + PBM live)

defi.label: DeFi Extension
defi.description: Adds on-chain protocol support (Uniswap, Aave, Curve, Hyperliquid DEX). Requires CeFi path complete first.
additional_libraries: unified-api-contracts (DeFi: Uniswap v3/v4, Aave v3, Curve, TheGraph, DeFiLlama)

sports.label: Sports Betting Vertical
sports.description: Sports betting as a separate vertical. Does NOT require DeFi. Can be done in parallel with CeFi post-commercialisation.
additional_libraries: unified-api-contracts (sports: api_football, FootyStats, soccer-football-info, Betfair, Pinnacle, Open Meteo)
additional_services: instruments-service (sports reference data module, merged from sports-reference-data-service)
market-data-processing-service (sports odds processing module, merged from sports-odds-processing-service)
strategy-service (sports strategy module, merged from sports-strategy-service)
execution-service (sports execution module, merged from sports-execution-service)
sports.note: Sports services consolidated 2026-03-01: sports-reference-data-service merged into instruments-service, sports-odds-processing-service merged into market-data-processing-service, sports-strategy-service merged into strategy-service, sports-execution-service merged into execution-service. features-sports-service and unified-sports-execution-interface remain standalone. Can develop sports ML pipeline in parallel with CeFi. Only needs USEI v1.0.0 for live execution.

deployment.description: L0 DAG — cloud provider, protocol defaults, messaging topology, colocation groups. Libraries own infra decisions; services declare mode + capabilities only.

local.note: CI, dev/test — no cloud SDKs required

messaging_topology.note: pubsub/sqs for async fan-out across service boundaries; redis for low-latency point-to-point; shared_memory for zero-copy within a colocated group

colocation_groups.note: Latency-critical path; must deploy together on same host; no network hop between them

infra_authority.note: Libraries (UCI T0, UTL T1, protocol router) own all infra decisions. Service source files must NEVER contain: gcs, s3, bigquery, pubsub, sqs, redis, bucket, dataset, topic, cloudtarget, upload_to_gcs, StandardizedDomainCloudService. Those belong only in UCI providers and deployment config.

data_flows.note: Direct service call, no API layer
Direct service call, no API layer

critical.description: Blocks trading or deployment. Failure means money at risk or services unreachable.
critical.coverage_rule: COV-001 — all 3 layers required (ui_mock + api_mock + real_flow)

high.description: Degrades UX or causes data staleness. Not immediately dangerous but impacts user confidence.
high.coverage_rule: COV-002 — ui_mock + api_mock required; real_flow recommended
high.failure_action: WARNING — logged and reported, does not block promotion

medium.description: Cosmetic or non-blocking. Navigation, branding, tooltips, non-critical table rendering.
medium.coverage_rule: COV-003 — at least one test in any layer
medium.failure_action: INFO — reported only, no point deduction

journeys.expected_ui_update: Status badge transitions to 'deploying', then 'deployed'
Kill switch badge toggles between 'active' and 'killed'
Confirmation toast shown, dropdown reflects new selection
Region badge updates to selected region
Log table filters to show only selected service entries
Events table renders with audit trail entries
Run status transitions to 'running', progress bar appears, results populate on completion
Results table populates with run_id, strategy, sharpe, net_alpha_bps, pnl columns
Alpha distribution chart and summary metrics update to reflect filtered category
Grid table renders with result_id, strategy, sharpe, net_alpha_bps, pnl, status badge
Submission result shows status (ACCEPTED/REJECTED) with instruction_id or error message
Live Positions table renders with symbol, side badge, qty, P&L, venue columns
Service health cards render with status badge, latency, uptime for each service
Alert History table renders with event_type, severity badge, service, timestamp
EOD Positions table renders with symbol, side badge, qty, entry/current price, unrealized P&L, settled status
Fund Invoicing table renders with client, amount, currency, status badge (paid/pending/overdue), due date
Browser initiates file download for finalized report
New venue appears in table with 'disconnected' status badge, dialog closes
New client appears in table with 'pending' status badge, 2-step wizard completes
New strategy appears in table with 'paused' status badge, dialog closes
Navigate to strategy detail page with Sharpe, daily P&L, monthly return, venue tags
Equity curve chart renders, summary cards show Sharpe, total return, max drawdown, trade count
Live badge pulses, P&L card updates every 5s, open positions table renders
Navigate to experiment detail with accuracy, loss, epochs, dataset, status badge
Deploy modal shows progress, model appears in models page with 'staging' status
Model status badge transitions from 'staging' to 'production'
Job table filters to show only failed jobs with error badge and progress bar
TTS Records table renders with service, event_type, tts_tag badge, resolved status
Service card expands to show GCS path table with status badges (present/missing/stale), size, updated timestamp
Recon table filters to failed runs, shows ML/Strategy/Execution stage badges and deviation count
Navigate to deviation drill page with field-level mismatches between batch and live
Order book refreshes with new symbol's bid/ask tables, mid price, spread update
P50/P95/P99/P99.9 summary cards render, venue breakdown table shows per-venue latency with status badges
Status text transitions from 'Generating...' to 'Report generated successfully.'
Monthly returns bar chart renders, performance table shows period, return %, AUM
Reports tab shows list of generated reports with period, type, status, download links

dependency_graph.description: Derived from pyproject.toml. Compare to workspace-manifest.json.

strategy.$note: Strategy UI fetches /api/strategies/* from execution-results-api. Backtest runs use a separate local Python process (port 5001), not an API service.

settlement.$note: Settlement routes live in trading-analytics-api (/settlement/* and /settlements/*). No separate settlement-api needed.

live-health-monitor.$note: Live health monitor calls /api/positions, /api/risk, /api/health from execution-results-api. Manual trade instructions route to execution-service directly (VITE_EXECUTION_API_URL).

logs-dashboard.$note: Logs dashboard calls /api/v1/audit/trail and /audit/trail/services from batch-audit-api. Manifest confirms batch-audit-api serves_ui includes logs-dashboard-ui.

odum-website.$note: Self-contained Next.js app with built-in API routes (Firebase Auth + Firestore). No separate API service. Uses port 3000 (Next.js default) instead of 5184 to match Next.js convention.

user-management.$note: Admin UI for user lifecycle management (onboard/modify/offboard). OAuth admin auth. Mock mode uses client-side fetch intercepts. Backend API TBD — currently calls external APIs (GitHub, Slack, MS Graph, GCP) via mock layer.
